design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from diverse character roster, progress through branching level paths fighting enemy waves, defeat stage bosses, unlock additional characters and endings
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat mastery against varied enemy types with character-specific strengths/weaknesses; navigation of branching paths that lead to different bosses and narrative outcomes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Urban vigilante justice against mega-corporation tyranny; personal revenge against DM Corporation for experimental atrocities; experimenting with diverse fighter archetypes (swift rushdown, grappler, ranged support, transforming powerhouse)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to character asymmetry and narrative branching in beat 'em up genre; each playable character has fundamentally different move sets, throw mechanics, and special attacks rather than cosmetic palette swaps; multiple endings and branching boss encounters tied to character selection and path choices; narrative focused on revenge-driven personal stories rather than world-saving heroics

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; character becomes vulnerable at low health
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; progress resets on game over
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through character selection—different characters have vastly different tool kits and learning curves; Echo is quick but weak, Al/Prokop are slow but powerful grapplers with ranged options, Flynn is balanced transformer

progression.upgrade_system: Character unlocking system: Norton becomes selectable after encountered in story; Orbot/M-Frame becomes selectable after encountered as mini-boss; no stat progression within runs
progression.unlock_mechanism: Narrative encounter triggers unlock additional playable characters; multiple endings unlock through branching path completion
replayability_hooks: Six distinct playable characters with fundamentally different movesets and playstyles
Multiple branching paths through stages leading to different boss encounters
Multiple endings tied to character selection and path choices
Character-specific special moves and throw animations
Versus mode with up to 4 players (with Multitap) offering competitive variety
Incentive to replay with each character to experience branching narrative variations

world_building_techniques: Detailed character backstories explaining DM Corporation wrongdoings and personal revenge motivations
Branching cutscenes that vary based on selected character and path taken
Environmental level design reflecting story context (Stalag 17 labs, Musashi Plaza urban setting)
Character references and cameos (Douglas Bild appears at level start; Lord J reference in North American version)
Localization incorporating literary/cultural references (Iago, Fnord, esoteric naming conventions)
Character-specific dialogue and narrative arcs within shared story framework

boss_design.philosophy: Branching boss encounters scaled to character capabilities; different bosses appear on different narrative paths; emphasizes character-specific interactions (Norton recognizes/confronts Lord J variant)
notable_bosses: Orbot/M-Frame mini-boss variants fought as regular enemies throughout game
Lord J reference boss (recognizable primarily to Norton through character-specific dialogue)

faction_indicators: DM Corporation: dystopian mega-corporation conducting genetic experimentation, controls world resources
Peace Keepers faction: Flynn, Echo, Al, Prokop united by personal grievances against DM
Military structure implied through Al's background and rank-based hierarchy
danger_signals: Resource monopolization and economic control post-2011 'economic wars'
Corporate enforcement through violent thugs and combat robots
Kidnapping and forced conscription (Rick's sister Maria, Prokop's sister Amy)
environmental_storytelling: Ruined DM laboratory setting (Stalag 17) suggesting active resistance/destruction
Urban street level (Snake Pliskin reference) representing civilian impact zones
Character equipment reflecting military/technical backgrounds (Al's bazooka, Orbot's blue flame/bullets)
Level progression through corporate infrastructure suggesting systematic dismantling of DM power
